content_body:
Located just 20 minutes from downtown Blue Ridge and on the Tennessee state line, McCaysville, GA & Copperhill, TN are known as the "Twin Cities" and is a must on your North Georgia bucket list! Originally settled in the early 1800s by pioneers and successful due to the copper boom, this small town is a popular destination for visitors and locals alike and boasts a large personality amid its quaint streets. In this article, we'll explore the charm of McCaysville and all it has to offer. Whether you visit this town on your own accord or via the Blue Ridge Scenic Railway, check out this nearby town on your next North Georgia getaway!

Exploring Downtown McCaysville

With its boutique shops, local restaurants, and friendly atmosphere, The Twin Cities are the perfect place to spend an afternoon! Take a stroll around town and find everything from antiques to specialty items. Explore the Riverwalk Shops and experience shops like Blue Ridge Adventure Wear, River Laurel Gifts, Christmas is Here!, The Pasta Market, and Deaf Man Vinyl! Whether you're with your girlfriends or family, the variety of boutiques promises something special for everyone.

While you shop till you drop, make sure to snap some photos at scenic locations like the classic State Line sign and Grand Street Bridge! Brag about being in two places at once as you bounce between McCaysville, GA and Copperhill, TN.

For those interested in the rich history behind the town, visit the Ducktown Basin Museum! Dive into the fascinating lore behind the Ducktown Basin, once the site of extensive copper mining operations. Gain a deeper understanding of the region's cultural heritage through the extensive artifacts housed at the museum.

Local Cuisine

No trip to McCaysville would be complete without trying one of the many dining options! From Southern comfort food to international flavors, there's something for everyone. Visit Burra Burra On the River for a classic menu, Twisted Tomato for fresh pizza, or Rum Cake Lady for authentic Cuban cuisine! For those with a sweet tooth, be sure to stop by Happy Bear for some homemade treats and ice cream. Satisfy your craving for a fresh brew by visiting Buck Bald Brewing! Enjoy the fresh air on their patio and sip their latest ale.

Twin City Nightlife

When the daylight begins to fade and the moon begins to rise, check out Bar 21 for some craft cocktails in an intimate, speakeasy-like atmosphere. Enjoy live music and a variety of wine and liquor to add a relaxing night cap to your fun-filled day.

If a more exciting location peaks your interest, visit Tooney's Music Venue! Located in the Riverwalk Shops, this hub for live music is 7,000 square feet and headlines local and well-known country artists which you can enjoy with an ice-cold beer in hand.

Outdoor Adventures

For those who love the great outdoors, McCaysville has plenty of things to do! The Toccoa River runs through the town (before turning into the Ocoee River), providing opportunities for fishing, kayaking, and tubing. Visit Horsheshoe Bend Park to enjoy nature and cast a line in the North Georgia Mountains. During the summer, visit this location for the seasonal concert series - Pickin' in the Park!

During the summer months, enjoy tubing down the Toccoa River with Toccoa River Tubing Company or Rolling Thunder River Company! Float down the peaceful river and enjoy the tranquil scenery that passes you by.

The Blue Ridge Scenic Railway offers a unique way to experience the beauty of the mountains, with its award-winning train ride that takes you from Blue Ridge to McCaysville, GA/Copperhill, TN. Enjoy an hour-long train ride each way with a two-hour layover in this small town.

Annual Events

One of the highlights of McCaysville is its annual events, namely the Fourth of July fireworks display, which has become a tradition for many families. The town also hosts a Christmas parade and a New Year's Eve celebration, making it a festive place to visit during the holiday season! 

Luxury Accommodations

Poised directly in between Blue Ridge and McCaysville are several of our luxury cabins! Whether Toccoa River access or mountain views suit your fancy, enjoy the best of both worlds at these Blue Ridge cabin rentals. Our 5-bedroom lodges boasts King/Queen suites, game rooms, hot tubs, and more amenities to bring your group together to enjoy a mountain vacation.

Fallen Timber Lodge

Whether you're looking for a weekend getaway or a place to call home, McCaysville/Copperhill has something for everyone. With its charming downtown, outdoor activities, annual events, delicious cuisine, and bustling nightlife, it's no wonder that this small town has developed in strides over the years. Come and explore the charm of McCaysville during your North Georgia getaway and discover why it's a hidden gem in the heart of the Blue Ridge Mountains!
title: McCaysville, GA: A Hidden Gem in the Blue Ridge Area
